rawHead:
<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>Schrödinger equation</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/contrib/auto-render.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[
        {left:'$$',right:'$$',display:true},
        {left:'$',right:'$',display:false},
        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},
        {left:'\\[',right:'\\]',display:true}
      ],
      throwOnError:false,
      macros:{
        '\\Spec':'\\Spec',
        '\\Gal':'\\Gal',
        '\\Hom':'\\Hom',
        '\\tr':'\\tr',
        '\\ad':'\\ad',
        '\\ind':'\\ind'
      }
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>
<!-- breadcrumb-head-auto-begin -->

rawBodyPrefix:
← Notebook
1 Time evolution 2 Stationary states 3 Harmonic oscillator 4 Hydrogen 5 Scattering 6 Path integral

Schrödinger equation

A self-adjoint Hamiltonian on $L^2$, a unitary one-parameter group, and the spectral theorem in disguise — quantum mechanics is functional analysis with a physical interpretation.

blocks.html:
1. Time-dependent Schrödinger equation

The state of a quantum particle is a unit vector $\psi \in L^2(\mathbb{R}^n)$, evolving by

$$ i\hbar\,\partial_t \psi = H\psi, \qquad H = -\frac{\hbar^2}{2m}\Delta + V(x). $$

$H$ is the Hamiltonian: kinetic energy $-\frac{\hbar^2}{2m}\Delta$ plus potential $V$. Self-adjointness of $H$ on its natural domain (Sobolev space $H^2$ for nice $V$) is the analytic content; once you have it, Stone's theorem hands you a one-parameter unitary group $U_t = e^{-itH/\hbar}$ and the solution is simply $\psi(t) = U_t \psi(0)$. Unitarity of $U_t$ gives the conservation law

$$ \tfrac{d}{dt}\|\psi(t)\|^2 = \tfrac{d}{dt}\langle \psi, \psi\rangle = \langle (-iH/\hbar)\psi,\psi\rangle + \langle \psi,(-iH/\hbar)\psi\rangle = 0, $$

so $\int |\psi(x,t)|^2\,dx \equiv 1$: total probability is preserved forever. Strip away the physics and Schrödinger evolution is a structure theorem: every self-adjoint operator generates a one-parameter unitary group, and that group is the dynamics.

Free wavepacket spreading
Slide $t$; the Gaussian's width grows as $\sqrt{1+t^2}$ but $\int|\psi|^2 = 1$.
t = 0.0
See also
  • Bounded operators and adjoints
  • Banach and Hilbert spaces
  • Classification: elliptic / parabolic / hyperbolic

See also
  • Bounded operators and adjoints
  • Banach and Hilbert spaces
  • Classification: elliptic / parabolic / hyperbolic

Used in
Schrödinger equation · Path integral and the classical limit
Schrödinger equation · Stationary states and the eigenvalue problem

2. Stationary states and the eigenvalue problem

Look for solutions of the special form $\psi(x,t) = e^{-iEt/\hbar}\varphi(x)$. Plugging into the time-dependent equation, the time derivative on the left gives $E\,\psi$, and the right gives $H\varphi$ times the same phase. Strip the phase:

$$ H\varphi = E\varphi. $$

This is the time-independent Schrödinger equation — an eigenvalue problem for a self-adjoint operator. By the spectral theorem, $H$ admits a resolution into eigenstates plus continuous-spectrum generalized eigenfunctions, and any state decomposes

$$ \psi = \sum_{n} c_n \varphi_n + \int c(E)\,\varphi_E\,dE. $$

The discrete sum runs over bound states — eigenvalues with $L^2$ eigenfunctions. The integral runs over scattering states — generalized eigenfunctions that are not in $L^2$ but participate in the spectral measure. A bound state evolves only by the phase $e^{-iE_n t/\hbar}$, so the probability density $|\varphi_n|^2$ doesn't change with time — that's why they're called stationary.

Superposition of two energy eigenstates
$\psi(x,t) = c_1 e^{-iE_1 t/\hbar}\varphi_1 + c_2 e^{-iE_2 t/\hbar}\varphi_2$. Watch $|\psi|^2$ beat at frequency $|E_2-E_1|/\hbar$.
|c₂|² = 0.50

See also
  • Spectral theorem (bounded self-adjoint)

Used in
Schrödinger equation · Harmonic oscillator and ladder operators
Schrödinger equation · Hydrogen atom
Schrödinger equation · Scattering states and barrier transmission

3. Harmonic oscillator and ladder operators

The simplest non-trivial Hamiltonian after the free particle is the harmonic oscillator,

$$ H = \frac{p^2}{2m} + \tfrac{1}{2}m\omega^2 x^2, \qquad p = -i\hbar\,\partial_x. $$

Define the dimensionless ladder operators

$$ a = \sqrt{\tfrac{m\omega}{2\hbar}}\Bigl(x + \tfrac{i}{m\omega}p\Bigr), \qquad a^\dagger = \sqrt{\tfrac{m\omega}{2\hbar}}\Bigl(x - \tfrac{i}{m\omega}p\Bigr). $$

The canonical commutator $[x,p] = i\hbar$ collapses to the textbook identity $[a,a^\dagger] = 1$, and a short calculation gives $H = \hbar\omega(a^\dagger a + \tfrac{1}{2})$. Now $a^\dagger a$ has non-negative eigenvalues, and $a$ lowers any eigenvalue by 1 while $a^\dagger$ raises it by 1. The lowest state $\varphi_0$ must be killed by $a$ (else lowering would produce a negative eigenvalue), and successive states are $\varphi_n = (a^\dagger)^n \varphi_0 / \sqrt{n!}$. The spectrum is

$$ E_n = \hbar\omega\bigl(n + \tfrac{1}{2}\bigr), \qquad n = 0, 1, 2, \dots $$

The eigenfunctions are Hermite polynomials times a Gaussian. The same algebra reappears wherever you see equally-spaced spectra: photons of frequency $\omega$ in QED, phonons in a crystal, second quantization in general.

Energy ladder $E_n = \hbar\omega(n+\tfrac{1}{2})$
Click a level to see its wavefunction (Hermite × Gaussian).

4. Hydrogen atom

For an electron in the Coulomb field of a proton, $V(r) = -e^2/r$. Spherical symmetry — $[H, L^2] = [H, L_z] = 0$ — means simultaneous eigenfunctions of $H, L^2, L_z$ exist, and separation of variables gives

$$ \varphi_{n\ell m}(r,\theta,\phi) = R_{n\ell}(r)\,Y_\ell^m(\theta,\phi), $$

with the angular part the spherical harmonics $Y_\ell^m$. The radial equation reduces to a 1D Schrödinger problem with effective potential $V_\mathrm{eff}(r) = -e^2/r + \hbar^2 \ell(\ell+1)/(2mr^2)$. Bound-state energies turn out to depend only on the principal quantum number $n = n_r + \ell + 1$, not on $\ell$:

$$ E_n = -\frac{E_0}{n^2}, \qquad E_0 = \frac{me^4}{2\hbar^2} \approx 13.6\,\mathrm{eV}. $$

The accidental degeneracy across $\ell$ at fixed $n$ — total multiplicity $n^2$ — is no accident: it comes from a hidden $\mathrm{SO}(4)$ symmetry generated by angular momentum together with the conserved Runge–Lenz vector. The continuous spectrum $[0, \infty)$ is the scattering regime: ionized electrons.

Hydrogen spectrum and Lyman/Balmer/Paschen series
$E_n = -13.6/n^2$ eV; transition $n\to m$ emits $|E_n - E_m|$.

See also
  • Laplace equation and the Dirichlet problem

5. Scattering states and barrier transmission

For a potential that decays at infinity, the spectrum of $H = -\frac{\hbar^2}{2m}\Delta + V$ above the asymptotic value of $V$ is purely continuous. The generalized eigenstates are plane-wave solutions $e^{ikx}$ — not in $L^2$, but participating in the spectral measure. Physically, they describe a particle of definite momentum $p = \hbar k$.

Send a wave $e^{ikx}$ from the left into a barrier. After it interacts with the potential it splits into a transmitted piece $T\,e^{ikx}$ on the right and a reflected piece $R\,e^{-ikx}$ on the left. Probability conservation — equivalently, unitarity of the S-matrix — gives

$$ |T|^2 + |R|^2 = 1. $$

The startling feature: even when the energy $E$ is below the barrier height $V_0$, transmission $|T|^2 > 0$. The wavefunction inside the barrier decays exponentially, $\psi(x) \propto e^{-\kappa x}$ with $\kappa = \sqrt{2m(V_0-E)}/\hbar$, but it doesn't reach zero, so a thin enough barrier always lets some amplitude through. This is quantum tunneling — the engine of $\alpha$-decay, the scanning tunneling microscope, and Josephson junctions.

Rectangular barrier: tunneling probability $T(E)$
Drag $E$ across $V_0$. Transmission stays non-zero on the classically forbidden side.
E/V₀ = 0.50
a = 1.5

See also
  • Spectrum of self-adjoint operators

6. Path integral and the classical limit

Feynman re-derived quantum mechanics in 1948 by writing the propagator $K(x_f, t_f; x_i, t_i) = \langle x_f | e^{-iH(t_f-t_i)/\hbar}| x_i\rangle$ as an integral over all paths $q(t)$ from $x_i$ to $x_f$:

$$ K(x_f, t_f; x_i, t_i) = \int_{q(t_i)=x_i}^{q(t_f)=x_f} e^{i S[q]/\hbar}\,\mathcal{D}q, \qquad S[q] = \int_{t_i}^{t_f} L(q,\dot q)\,dt. $$

Each path is weighted by the phase $e^{iS/\hbar}$ where $S$ is the classical action. The Schrödinger equation is recovered by deriving the propagator's evolution from short-time slices. The integral is heuristic — there's no honest Lebesgue measure $\mathcal{D}q$ on path space — but rigorous via Wick rotation $t \to -i\tau$, which converts the oscillatory integral into the Wiener integral over Brownian paths.

The classical limit is geometric: as $\hbar \to 0$, the phase $e^{iS/\hbar}$ oscillates faster and faster, and contributions from neighboring paths cancel — except where $\delta S = 0$. The stationary points of $S$ are exactly the solutions of the Euler–Lagrange equation. So quantum mechanics says: take all paths; classical mechanics says: take only the stationary one. Stationary phase makes that the same statement at $\hbar \to 0$.

Stationary phase: $\hbar \to 0$ classical limit
Lower $\hbar$; only the path with $\delta S = 0$ (yellow) survives.
ℏ = 0.60

Connections

The Schrödinger equation is the meeting point of three frameworks: the spectral theorem (eigenstates as the diagonalization of $H$), elliptic PDE theory (regularity and self-adjoint extensions of $-\Delta + V$), and the path-integral / functional-integral approach that powers quantum field theory. Open frontiers: rigorous many-body Schrödinger operators (Coulomb stability of matter, BCS theory), nonlinear Schrödinger equations (Bose–Einstein condensates, soliton dynamics), and the still-mysterious measurement problem in the foundations.

  • Spectral theory — unbounded self-adjoint operators — the rigorous home of $-\Delta + V$ and Stone's theorem $H \leftrightarrow e^{-itH/\hbar}$.
  • Functional analysis — Hilbert spaces — $L^2$ as the state space, where the inner product encodes probabilities $|\langle\varphi,\psi\rangle|^2$.
  • PDE classification — Schrödinger sits between parabolic (heat) and hyperbolic (wave) by the imaginary unit, with infinite-speed propagation but conservation of $L^2$.
  • Harmonic analysis — the Fourier transform diagonalizes the free Hamiltonian and underlies the momentum representation.
  • Operator algebras — observables as a $C^*$-algebra; states as positive linear functionals; the Heisenberg-picture dual to wave mechanics.
